─[ Cold/Warm A/B Test — Delegation + Knowledge Reuse ]─ ┐

Cold/Warm A/B Test

Measuring the cost of getting the answer vs capturing knowledge, and how captured knowledge pays for itself on subsequent runs. 50 Claude Code sessions across 5 conditions, same task, same day.

Headline Results

ConditionAnswer CostAnswer TimeCapture Timevs Raw Cold
Raw Cold (baseline)$0.451m 12s——
Lore Cold$0.321m 22s0m 35scost -29%
Lore Warm$0.241m 4s—cost -46%, time -11%
Lore Hot$0.240m 57s0m 37s *cost -46%, time -21%
Lore Runbook$0.190m 40s—cost -59%, time -44%

!!! danger "" * Lore Hot capture time is operator-initiated runbook creation — the operator explicitly asked the agent to write a runbook after getting the answer. This is not automatic framework behavior.

Total capture investment: $0.17 — pays back in less than 1 runbook run.

Documents

FileContents
analysis.mdFindings, cost progression, capture ROI, variance analysis, cumulative economics, mechanisms
methodology.mdTest design, conditions, pricing model, timing rules, cost-split method, token deduplication
operator-inputs.mdExact operator messages per condition, extracted from session transcripts
all-sessions-raw-data.mdFull per-session token, cache, model, orchestrator/agent, and cost breakdown for all 50 sessions

Setup

  • Platform: Claude Code (CLI)
  • Task: “See if we have enough inventory to fill all orders from last week.”
  • Services: lore-gotcha-demo mock APIs (orders + inventory with deliberate API gotchas)
  • Date: 2026-02-22
  • Models: Opus 4.6 orchestrator, Haiku 4.5 workers (Raw Cold uses Opus inline for everything)

Conditions

ConditionNWorkspaceFrameworkPrior Knowledge
Raw Cold10test/control-01NoneNone
Lore Cold10test/lore-01..10Lore v0.11.0None
Lore Warm10test/lore-01..10Lore v0.11.0Skills + env docs from cold
Lore Hot10test/lore-01..10Lore v0.11.0Skills + env docs, writes runbook
Lore Runbook10test/lore-01..10Lore v0.11.0Full knowledge + runbook

Limitations

  • One task type. API exploration with mock services. Results may differ for code generation, refactoring, or debugging.
  • One model family. Claude (Opus 4.6 orchestrator, Haiku 4.5 workers). Other models may differ.
  • Subscription pricing. Tests ran on Claude Max. Cost figures are API-equivalent estimates.
  • No cross-tool validation. Knowledge captured on Claude Code should benefit Cursor and OpenCode — not yet tested.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
